check: Does the /review skill instruct reading the reflect log for unreviewed entries since the last weekly summary?
Does /review present concepts eligible for promotion with current confidence, suggested level, and a recommendation to promote or defer?
Does /review support triage actions including promote, dismiss, and defer (or skip) for signals presented to the user?
Does /review run confidence decay after triage to catch concepts that have gone stale?
Does /review create a weekly summary record in concepts.db via a CLI command like review-summary?
Does /review write a synthesis markdown file to a configurable weekly directory path?
Does the weekly synthesis format include a 'What moved' or equivalent section showing actual progress across projects?
Does the weekly synthesis format include a 'What the patterns say' or 'Patterns' section covering friction, convergence, and dormant signals?
Does the weekly synthesis include a section showing concept graph changes: new concepts, strengthened edges, and conflicting edges?
Does the weekly synthesis include a drift check section comparing stated goals against actual work activity, listing goals with no work and overdue revisit decisions?
Does the weekly synthesis include a carry-forward section listing unresolved signals from the reflect log or deferred items?
Does the weekly synthesis include a suggested focus section with a recommendation for next week?
Does the weekly synthesis include delta comparisons to the previous week for concepts, edges, or projects?
Does /review surface stale concepts that have been unreferenced for 14 or more days for user awareness?
Does /review use ~/.cortex/concepts (absolute tilde path) for all CLI invocations rather than relying on PATH?
Does /review read .memory-config for path configuration with fallback defaults?
Does /review read and triage entries from a correction queue file (such as correction-queue.json) that contains flagged concepts from the graph explorer?
Does /review require explicit user confirmation before promoting concepts, never auto-promoting?
Does the /review completion report include current graph status (concepts, edges, projects) and weekly change delta?
Does /review track deferred items in the synthesis for carry-forward to the next week?
Does the drift check in the weekly synthesis specifically compare goals from a learnings or goals file against actual work activity in daily notes or graph changes?
Does the drift check identify decisions marked as revisit that are older than 14 days?
Does /review specify clearing processed entries from the correction queue file after triage?
Does /review Step 2 gather at least four distinct types of signals (such as promotion-eligible, stale concepts, shared/cross-project concepts, and hot concepts) via separate CLI queries?
Does the /review completion report include the count of corrections processed alongside promotions, dismissals, and deferrals?
Does the /review completion report indicate when drift is detected between stated goals and actual work?
Does /review specify different behavior or messaging for the first review (when there is no prior weekly summary to compare against for deltas)?
Does /review specify what happens if the reflect log has no entries or no unreviewed entries? Does it still produce a weekly synthesis?
Does /review specify what happens if the concepts CLI (~/.cortex/concepts) is not installed or not available? Does it fail gracefully?
Does /review define how WEEK_START is calculated (e.g., Monday of the current week) so the weekly directory file naming is deterministic?
Does /review reference the confidence lifecycle thresholds: tentative to established (3+ sources or 2+ projects) and established to settled (strength >= 5 and age >= 30 days)?

Does /review specify confidence decay thresholds: settled demotes to established after 90 days unreferenced, and established demotes to tentative after 60 days unreferenced, with tentative as the floor?
Does /review specify at least two concrete correction actions for queued items: rename (via concepts correct) and merge (via concepts merge)?
Does the 'What moved' section of the synthesis specify that it should cover actual progress across projects (not just graph statistics)?
Does /review handle the case where the weekly directory ($WEEKLY_DIR) does not yet exist, either by creating it or by specifying the expected behavior?
Does the weekly synthesis include stale concepts in the carry-forward or a dedicated section for awareness, rather than only listing them during interactive triage?
If /review is run twice in the same week, does the document address whether it overwrites the existing synthesis file or appends? Is the behavior deterministic?
Does /review ensure that all graph modifications (promotions, dismissals, corrections) require explicit user confirmation before executing CLI commands that change concepts.db?
Does /review write the weekly summary to BOTH a markdown file ($WEEKLY_DIR) and concepts.db (via review-summary CLI), not just one destination?
Does /review clearly distinguish which signals can be promoted (concepts) versus dismissed (edges) versus deferred (any signal type)? Are the CLI commands for each action different?
Does /review never create new concepts in the graph? It should only promote, dismiss, decay, or correct existing concepts, not add new ones.
Does /review consistently reference 'reflect' (not 'dream') when referring to the consolidation log or the companion skill?
